name: Monkfish with Almonds, Sweet Red Peppers & Sa
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Monkfish, in large whole filets, skin and membranes removed
3 large Red bell peppers, cored, seeded, cut into strips lengthwise
1/2 tsp White pepper, or to taste
1 Ounce sliced almonds, toasted, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Bake the fish in a buttered baking pan for 15 minutes. Let cool.
3. Cut into medallions and set aside. Reserve the baking juice.
4. Heat the oil in a large skillet, add the garlic and peppers, and sauté over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Grind whole almonds finely in a food processor and add to the skillet with the saffron, wine, stock, reserved fish juices, salt, and pepper. Cook over medium heat for 5 minutes.
6. Transfer the sauce to a blender or food processor and puree. Return the sauce to the skillet, add the fish medallions, and heat through.
7. Serve immediately, with sliced almonds sprinkled on top. Serves 6. PER SERVING: 370 calories, 34 g protein, 8 g carbohydrate, 23 g fat (3 g saturated), 103 mg cholesterol, 288 mg sodium, 3 g fiber.
